title: How Computers Work
description: Understand CPU execution, memory registers, pointers, caches, and instructions from a low-level perspective.
learningObjectives: Explain the Von Neumann architecture (CPU, RAM, I/O)
Describe registers, the program counter, and the Fetch-Decode-Execute cycle
Identify how C++ code maps to CPU machine instructions

sections.title: The CPU and RAM Hierarchy
sections.content: At the hardware level, a computer consists of a Central Processing Unit (CPU) executing instructions from Random Access Memory (RAM). Because fetching from RAM is slow, CPUs contain tiny, ultra-fast memory storage units called Registers (like the Program Counter which tracks the active instruction address) and caches (L1, L2, L3) to speed up data execution flows.

sections.title: Mapping Code to Assembly Instructions
sections.content:
A high-level C++ statement like adding values translates directly to assembly CPU commands:

```assembly
; C++: int x = a + b;
MOV EAX, [a]  ; Load value of 'a' into accumulator register EAX
ADD EAX, [b]  ; Add value of 'b' to EAX register
MOV [x], EAX  ; Store result back to memory address of 'x'
```
